projects.still_open_because: The general report vs no-report NCC logic is well-trodden — Frässle et al. 2014 (10.1523/JNEUROSCI.4627-13.2014) showed frontal activity in binocular rivalry tracks report/introspection rather than perception, and Tsuchiya et al. 2015 TICS (10.1016/j.tics.2015.10.002) codified the no-report paradigm; Cogitate/adversarial-collaboration work (10.1038/s41586-025-08888-1) revisits frontal vs posterior NCC with report-free conditions. But all of these operate on presence/absence of a percept (rivalry, masking, face/object visibility), not on decodable low-level content. Searches turned up only the data descriptor itself for ds005684 (10.1038/s41597-025-04511-0, preprint 10.1101/2024.05.16.594267), with no citing analysis papers indexed. So three specific deltas remain: (1) applying the report/no-report contrast to hue-identity MVPA/RSA rather than to visibility, i.e. asking which regions carry *color content* in both conditions; (2) treating the trial-wise circle/square cue as a genuine within-subject randomized manipulation and estimating a causal (not merely descriptive) report effect; (3) the formal mediation test of whether prefrontal activity accounts for the report-related change in ventral hue decoding — no located work runs a mediation model linking PFC to content-decoding strength. Adjacent enough that framing must cite Frässle/Tsuchiya, but the content-level causal + mediation analysis on this dataset appears undone.
projects.dataset_name: Color Qualia Similarity Judgment fMRI Dataset (OpenNeuro ds005684)

projects.question_en: Is the individualized functional rTMS target also structurally connected to subgenual ACC, i.e. does DTI-derived white-matter connectivity from the target voxel to sgACC agree with the resting-state functional target in the same subject?
projects.question_es: ¿El blanco funcional individualizado de rTMS está además conectado estructuralmente al CCA subgenual, es decir, la conectividad de sustancia blanca derivada de DTI entre el vóxel blanco y el CCA subgenual coincide con el blanco funcional de reposo en el mismo sujeto?
projects.still_open_because: The core comparison has already been done in adjacent form. Vila-Rodriguez-adjacent work "Reproducible routes: reliably navigating the connectome to enrich personalized brain stimulation strategies" (Front Hum Neurosci 2024, doi:10.3389/fnhum.2024.1477049) derived, in the same individuals and at two timepoints, (1) FC-guided DLPFC targets from resting-state fMRI (sgACC-anticorrelation approach) and (2) SC-guided DLPFC targets from DTI tractography, and explicitly quantified intra-individual differences between the two target maps, concluding SC-guided targets are more reproducible. Related priors: Luber et al. 2022 (doi:10.1016/j.neuroimage.2021.118863) on DTI-based targeting of deep structures from cortical TMS sites, and Brainsight-era work on cingulum-bundle DLPFC-sgACC tracts and rTMS outcome (e.g. doi:10.1038/s41386-021-01256-3; doi:10.1016/j.jad.2021.12.005). Remaining delta specific to ds008064: (a) no published study has run the structural-vs-functional target concordance analysis in a treatment-resistant depression cohort where the functional target was actually used to deliver clinical rTMS, so the prior work never links target discordance to clinical response; (b) prior work compared *optimal target locations*, not the narrower question posed here — whether the actually-stimulated functional target voxel has non-trivial DTI streamline connectivity to sgACC in that same subject (a per-subject validity check rather than a target-generation comparison); (c) ds008064 pairs this with a psychotherapy (SST) vs mindfulness arm, allowing structure-function target agreement to be tested as a moderator of combined-treatment outcome, which no prior work has done. Confirm ds008064 actually ships usable DWI (with sufficient directions/b-values) before committing — if it is a low-angular-resolution DTI sequence, the tractography arm may be too weak to distinguish agreement from noise.
projects.dataset_name: Individualized fMRI-guided rTMS combined with Self-System Therapy versus mindfulness exercises in treatment-resistant depression (OpenNeuro ds008064)

projects.question_en: Can a pretrained fMRI foundation model produce embeddings of these resting-state scans stable enough that a session-1 scan can be re-identified from its session-2 counterpart, and do those embeddings beat conventional connectome features in a few-shot pre-versus-post classification?
projects.question_es: ¿Puede un modelo de fundación de fMRI preentrenado producir representaciones de estos escaneos en reposo lo bastante estables como para reidentificar un escaneo de sesión 1 a partir de su homólogo de sesión 2, y superan esas representaciones a los rasgos convencionales de conectoma en una clasificación pre-frente-a-post de pocos ejemplos?
projects.still_open_because:
Both halves of the question have close, recent antecedents, so the framing is not new — but neither has been done on longitudinal clinical treatment data.

(1) Embedding-vs-connectome head-to-head is largely pre-answered, and negatively. "The Variance Brain Foundation Models Forgot: Third-Order Statistics Predict Cognition Where Billion-Parameter Models Fail" (arXiv:2606.04010, May 2026) tests three state-of-the-art brain foundation models (incl. BrainLM 111M and 650M) against a plain linear readout of the ~80K-parameter FC matrix and finds BFM embeddings lose on every dataset, parcellation and readout, with the gap *widening* with model scale; it attributes this to pretraining destroying the third-order co-skewness structure. So "do foundation-model embeddings beat conventional connectome features" already has a published answer for phenotype prediction. Related benchmark work: "Foundation models for discovering robust biomarkers of neurological disorders from dynamic functional connectivity", "A Brain Graph Foundation Model", AdaBrain-Bench.

(2) Embedding-based fingerprinting/re-identification is also established, just not with a pretrained foundation model: Finn-style connectome fingerprinting plus learned-embedding versions — "Functional connectome fingerprinting: identifying individuals and predicting cognitive functions via autoencoder" (10.1002/hbm.25394), the deep-learning variant on arXiv, tangent-space fingerprinting (10.1162/netn_a_00445), and stability-over-months work (10.1523/JNEUROSCI.0480-21.2021).

Specific remaining delta: (a) no paper found that reports session-1/session-2 identification rate for embeddings from an off-the-shelf pretrained fMRI foundation model (BrainLM / Brain-JEPA / BrainMass class) used as an embedding-QC criterion; the BFM-vs-FC literature evaluates phenotype prediction, not identifiability. (b) No work on ds008064 (Duke fMRI-guided rTMS + Self-System Therapy TRD trial; only conventional network analyses exist, e.g. 10.1016/j.ijchp.2023.100382, 10.1097/YCT.0000000000000545). (c) Few-shot *within-subject pre- vs post-treatment state* classification — a different target from the cross-subject cognition/diagnosis prediction that all BFM benchmarks use — is untested.

Caveat for the student: given arXiv:2606.04010, the expected result on the comparison arm is that FC wins, and ds008064 is a small trial (n in the tens, two sessions), so the few-shot arm will likely be underpowered to publish as a positive claim. Framing it as a clinical-longitudinal reliability/negative-result replication of the BFM-vs-FC finding is the defensible angle; framing it as a novel discovery is not.
projects.dataset_name: Individualized fMRI-guided rTMS combined with Self-System Therapy versus mindfulness exercises in treatment-resistant depression (OpenNeuro ds008064)

projects.question_en: In refractory status epilepticus requiring continuous IV anaesthesia, does earlier initiation (within 24 h of seizure onset) versus later initiation change in-hospital mortality, ventilator-free days and discharge disposition, framed as a target-trial emulation with propensity adjustment for admission severity and aetiology?
projects.question_es: En el estado epiléptico refractario que requiere anestesia intravenosa continua, ¿el inicio precoz (en las primeras 24 h desde el comienzo de las crisis) frente al inicio tardío modifica la mortalidad intrahospitalaria, los días libres de ventilador y el destino al alta, planteado como emulación de ensayo diana con ajuste por puntaje de propensión sobre gravedad al ingreso y etiología?
projects.clinical_rationale: Guidelines are silent on the timing of continuous anaesthesia and clinicians disagree whether aggressive early burst-suppression protects brain or merely commits fragile patients to prolonged ventilation and its complications.
projects.outcome_measure: In-hospital mortality; secondary: ventilator-free days and discharge disposition (home vs facility)
projects.still_open_because: The specific remaining delta is methodological and one outcome: (1) a formal target-trial emulation design with an explicit 24-h time-zero cutoff and immortal-time handling, rather than the exposure-category propensity matching used by the 2024 study; and (2) ventilator-free days as an outcome, which no identified study reports for this exposure. These are incremental refinements, not a new clinical question — the direction of effect (no mortality/functional benefit from earlier anaesthesia) is already established.

projects.question_en: Because task-SCT contains an experimenter-manipulated matched control condition, what is the within-subject effect of the creative-generation manipulation on BOLD response and its temporal dynamics across a run, and how much of that effect survives when order, run number and time-on-task are modeled as confounds?
projects.question_es: Dado que la task-SCT incluye una condición de control emparejada manipulada experimentalmente, ¿cuál es el efecto intrasujeto de la manipulación de generacion creativa sobre la respuesta BOLD y su dinámica temporal a lo largo del run, y cuánto de ese efecto persiste al modelar el orden, el número de run y el tiempo en tarea como confusores?
projects.still_open_because: The dataset is OpenNeuro ds008055 "Scientific Creativity MRI Dataset" (Luchini & Beaty, 2026; task-rest + task-SCT), and the primary analysis of exactly this paradigm is already published: Beaty, Cortes, Merseal, Hardiman & Green, "Brain networks supporting scientific creative thinking," Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts (2025), DOI 10.1037/aca0000603 — ~47 STEM undergraduates, scientific hypothesis-generation blocks contrasted against an experimenter-matched synonym-generation control task, with univariate contrasts plus whole-brain MVPA identifying default (PCC), salience (right anterior insula) and executive-control contributions. So the core "within-subject effect of the creative-generation manipulation on BOLD" is PUBLISHED and re-deriving it is largely replication. The remaining delta is the second half of the question: (a) the within-run TEMPORAL DYNAMICS of the generation-vs-control effect (time-resolved/parametric time-in-block or time-in-run modulation of the contrast) is not reported in aca0000603 — the closest work is a different lab/paradigm (verbal divergent thinking, "The time course of creativity: multivariate classification of default and executive network contributions over time," Cortex 2022, S0010945222002404), which used a different task and dataset; and (b) no published analysis quantifies how much of the SCT generation effect SURVIVES when block/condition order, run number and cumulative time-on-task are entered as nuisance regressors — i.e. a confound-robustness / sensitivity analysis of the published effect. Framing the project as "temporal dynamics + confound-hardening of an already-published contrast" is defensible; framing it as discovering the generation-vs-control effect is not.

projects.question_en: Which early developmental trajectories predict the onset of psychiatric disorders in adolescence?
projects.question_es: ¿Trayectorias tempranas que predigan aparición de trastornos psiquiátricos en la adolescencia?

projects.question_en: How reproducible are brain-behaviour associations once realistic effect sizes are assumed?
projects.question_es: ¿Reproducibilidad de asociaciones cerebro-conducta con tamaños de efecto realistas?

projects.question_en: Do MCI-to-AD conversion models generalise to under-represented populations, across both ethnicity and scanner diversity?
projects.question_es: ¿Generalizan los modelos de conversión a poblaciones no representadas (diversidad étnica y de escáner)?

projects.question_en: Can passive or digital biomarkers substitute for, or complement, expensive imaging?
projects.question_es: ¿Biomarcadores digitales/pasivos que complementen la imagen costosa?

projects.question_en: Are calibration-free, plug-and-play decoders achievable that still work outside the lab?
projects.question_es: ¿Decoders plug-and-play sin calibración por sujeto que funcionen en el mundo real?

projects.question_en: Can radiomic signatures predict molecular subtype or MGMT methylation without biopsy, robustly across centres and scanners?
projects.question_es: ¿Firmas radiómicas que predigan subtipo molecular o metilación MGMT sin biopsia, robustas entre centros y escáneres?

projects.question_en: Does tumour segmentation generalise to post-surgical and low-field MRI?
projects.question_es: ¿Segmentación que generalice a resonancias postquirúrgicas y de bajo campo?

projects.question_en: Can seizure prediction generalise across patients (cross-subject) without per-patient retraining?
projects.question_es: ¿Predicción de crisis que generalice entre pacientes (cross-subject) sin reentrenar?

projects.question_en: Which models generalise from one hospital to another, and which collapse under site shift?
projects.question_es: ¿Qué modelos generalizan de un hospital a otro y cuáles colapsan por cambio de dominio (site shift)?

projects.question_en: Are there transdiagnostic neuroimaging signatures that cut across classical diagnostic boundaries?
projects.question_es: ¿Firmas de neuroimagen transdiagnósticas que crucen fronteras diagnósticas clásicas?

projects.question_en: Can acute neurological deterioration (delirium, haemorrhage) be predicted by combining clinical notes with waveform signals, and does it externally validate on eICU?
projects.question_es: ¿Predecir deterioro neurológico agudo (delirium, hemorragia) integrando notas + señales, con validación externa en eICU?

projects.question_en: Do clinical foundation models pre-trained on MIMIC transfer few-shot to neurosurgical tasks?
projects.question_es: ¿Modelos de fundación clínicos preentrenados en MIMIC que transfieran few-shot a tareas neuroquirúrgicas?

projects.question_en: Which architectures genuinely generalise across datasets and paradigms, and which merely overfit the benchmark?
projects.question_es: ¿Qué arquitecturas generalizan realmente entre datasets y paradigmas, y cuáles sobreajustan al benchmark?

projects.question_en: Can brain-age models be calibrated well enough to be clinically actionable at the individual level?
projects.question_es: ¿Modelos de brain-age calibrados que sean clínicamente accionables a nivel individual?
projects.dataset_name: OASIS (Open Access Series of Imaging Studies)

projects.question_en: Do foundation models trained on the aggregate BIDS corpus transfer across tasks and populations?
projects.question_es: ¿Modelos de fundación entrenados sobre el agregado BIDS que transfieran entre tareas y poblaciones?

projects.question_en: How reproducible are published clinical findings when reanalysed with standardised pipelines?
projects.question_es: ¿Reproducibilidad de hallazgos clínicos al reanalizar con pipelines estandarizados?

projects.question_en: Do shared genetic factors translate into common circuits that neuromodulation can treat?
projects.question_es: ¿Los factores genéticos compartidos se traducen en circuitos comunes tratables por neuromodulación?

projects.question_en: Can genetic stratification predict response to medication, or to TMS/DBS?
projects.question_es: ¿Estratificación genética que prediga respuesta a fármacos o a TMS/DBS?

projects.question_en: Are there multimodal biomarkers that predict subthalamic DBS response when stratified by genotype (GBA vs LRRK2)?
projects.question_es: ¿Biomarcadores multimodales que predigan respuesta a DBS subtalámico estratificados por genotipo (GBA vs LRRK2)?

projects.question_en: Can trajectory models anticipate conversion from prodromal to manifest Parkinson's disease?
